public_reputation: Known locally through day-to-day work as quartermaster.
appearance: Korin Ashmason carries the marks of quartermaster in industrial grit: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Sounds hospitable until terms blur, then becomes sharply attentive to cost and precedent. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a quartermaster still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of quartermaster in and around Ashforge Quarry, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Ashforge Quarry while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to work shortfalls is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Korin Ashmason will trade route, scarcity, or faction insight once the party resolves a commercial problem others cannot touch openly.
Korin Ashmason needs help protecting a shipment, recovering leverage, or stabilizing exchange around Ashforge Quarry before trust collapses.

support_scope_notes: Promoted from a preexisting named interface into the unified full-content NPC architecture. Sparse unsupported fields remain intentionally restrained.
public_signal: Textiles, tokens, tally habits, and practiced bargaining poise make Korin Ashmason read as someone who lives by exchange and reputation. In Ashforge Quarry, this lands inside a public field already shaped by Hill Giant foremen, industrial clans.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Ashforge Quarry: markets, counting tables, depots, ports, inns, or contract rooms
who_avoids_them: those who waste time without value

first_impression: Korin Ashmason presents as Sounds hospitable until terms blur, then becomes sharply attentive to cost and precedent. In Soreheim Alliance, that reads as someone quota-aware, contribution-minded, and structurally pragmatic, filtering choices through communal ambition, and the habits of a quartermaster still shape how they handle timing, silence, and attention. and immediately reads for leverage, obligation, and risk.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for Quartermaster, and proof that strangers will not create avoidable noise in Soreheim Alliance.
what_they_refuse: Korin Ashmason refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing Quartermaster too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Korin Ashmason can open doors, explain local expectations, or redirect trouble before it reaches a harder authority layer.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Korin Ashmason adds delay, scrutiny, and reputational drag before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Korin Ashmason is Known locally through day-to-day work as quartermaster. and rarely acts without a second motive.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or mercy from a Quartermaster.
social_texture: Korin Ashmason speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Ashforge Quarry, people read them as quota-aware, contribution-minded, and never fully off the clock.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to production; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but work shortfalls becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Korin touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Korin Ashmason when the party needs a socially grounded face for Ashforge Quarry: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
